title: Reply with exactly: reviewer OK
summary_text: Current git state I inspected for `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public`: - Branch: `xauto-prod-verify-gha` (tracks `origin/xauto-prod-verify-gha`, `+0/-0`) - Dirty: `128` modified tracked files, `223` untracked files - Staged: none - Stash: empty - Noise concentration: mostly `.fugue/*` runtime artifacts Safest non-destructive cleanup strategy (do this exact sequence, no resets/cleans): ```bash repo=/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public ts=$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) backup_dir=/Users/masayuki/Documents/fugue-wip-backup-$ts # 1) Immutable safety backup outside repo (tracked + untracked) mkdir -p "$backup_dir" git -C "$repo" status --porcelain=v2 --branch > "$backup_dir/status.porcelain-v2.txt" git -C "$repo" diff --binary > "$backup_dir/tracked-working.patch" git -C "$repo" ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z \ | tar --null -T - -czf "$backup_dir/untracked-files.tgz" -C "$repo" # optional: full filesystem snapshot of repo working tree tar -czf "$backup_dir/repo-working-tree.tgz" -C /Users/masayuki/Dev fugue-orchestrator-public # 2) Park everything in stash (includes untracked + ignored) to clean original worktree git -C "$repo" stash push --all -m "safety-full-worktree-$ts" # 3) Verify original worktree is orderly/clean git -C "$repo" status --short # 4) Keep original clean; continue edits in separate worktree git -C "$repo" worktree add /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public-wip-$ts -b wip/$ts git -C /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public-wip-$ts stash apply stash@{0} # 5) Verify stash is still retained (do not pop yet) git -C "$repo" stash list | sed -n '1,5p' ``` Why this is safest: - Two independent backups before any cleanup. - `stash apply` (not `pop`) keeps a recovery copy. - Original worktree becomes clean/orderly. - No destructive commands (`reset --hard`, `clean -fdx`, checkout discard) needed.
